summary: This iteration validates, against the live /target oracle and truth caches on six dev scenarios, whether a formula-guided strategy should seed the maximum-RPS search at the UPPER endpoint of the iter-2 per-cell bracket [M_ITL, min(M_TPF,256)] rather than the LOWER endpoint (M_ITL) chosen in iter-2. The core claim is that the throughput plateau onset M* lies at or above M_ITL, so the upper endpoint lands on the flat plateau and achieves near-zero throughput gap in a single oracle call.
key_points: Four strategy arms run through the same execution path (python -m nous.harness.run), each scored on (oracle calls, gap_throughput_rel). They differ only in where the search is seeded: h-main seeds the bracket UPPER endpoint; h-ablation swaps to the LOWER endpoint M_ITL; h-control-negative uses a constant M_MAX=256 with no formula; h-robustness examines the ttft-only edge case.

CONFIRM h-main: seed_upper achieves gap_throughput_rel <= ~0.005 on all six scenarios at exactly 1 oracle call, never worse than seed_lower, with the largest improvement on high-occupancy scenarios (alpha-high, itl-only, ratio M_truth/M_ITL ~2.1-2.8) and smallest on alpha-low (~1.6).

CONFIRM h-ablation: switching to the lower endpoint M_ITL strictly inflates the throughput gap on the four ITL-binding scenarios (baseline 69/40, itl-only 17/8, alpha-low 170/107, alpha-high 17/6) while leaving ttft-only and unbounded unchanged — isolating the upper endpoint as the load-bearing component.

CONFIRM h-control-negative (the crux for scoping iter-4/5): constant M_MAX=256 matches seed_upper to float noise on all six dev scenarios because under nc=1 the throughput plateau extends all the way to M=256 (peak-to-f(256) drop <= 0.0027). This means the dev set CANNOT distinguish a precise formula from a trivial M_MAX seed — formula precision only pays off when nc>1 breaks the plateau (iter-5 benchmark).

CONFIRM h-robustness: for ttft-only, M_truth=92 lies ABOVE the bracket upper endpoint (M_TPF=70), so the bracket fails to contain the true argmax — yet seed_upper's gap stays near zero, correcting the iter-2/RP-5 belief that M_TPF upper-bounds the throughput argmax (it only bounds the TTFT constraint crossing).

REFUTATION signals to watch: any scenario where seed_upper's gap exceeds seed_lower's (non-monotone f, peak-then-decline — recheck nc); seed_upper returning infeasible M (throughput 0, gap ~1.0 — queue wait makes upper endpoint infeasible); or naive_max materially worse than seed_upper (plateau breaks before M=256, promoting formula precision earlier than expected).
